mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals an accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 130k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7%) indicates a tightening labor market that supports corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS > 130k and UNRATE < 7% fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect's t-statistic drops below 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 850k) and retail sales (RSAFS > 410k) signal strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST > 850k and RSAFS > 410k condition fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO > 97.5) reflects robust manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict positive 5d SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the correlation coefficient between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent disinflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.5%) reduces real interest rate uncertainty, encouraging equity investment and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5%, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2% annualized) in a late-cycle expansion phase signals sustained corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or the regression coefficient for GDPC1 becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 7%) indicates labor market tightness that supports wage growth and corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE remains below 7%, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO > 97.5) during a post-shock recovery phase indicates manufacturing resilience, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or the correlation coefficient between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below 0.1.
